World Development Indicators, 2022 (country-level official statistics)

Source: R/data.R
wdi2022.Rd

A cross-section of four official development indicators for 207 countries and territories in 2022, retrieved from the World Bank's World Development Indicators (WDI) API. The dataset is used by the package vignettes as a realistic official-statistics example for mixture clustering (countries group into latent development regimes) and mixture-of-regressions (the Preston-curve relationship between national income and life expectancy).

Usage

wdi2022

Format

A data frame with 207 rows and 9 columns:

country

Country or territory name (World Bank).

iso3

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code.

region

World Bank geographic region.

income

World Bank income classification.

gdp_pc

GDP per capita, constant 2015 US$ (indicator NY.GDP.PCAP.KD).

life_exp

Life expectancy at birth, total, years (SP.DYN.LE00.IN).

fertility

Total fertility rate, births per woman (SP.DYN.TFRT.IN).

urban

Urban population, percent of total (SP.URB.TOTL.IN.ZS).

year

Observation year (2022 for all rows).

Source

World Bank, World Development Indicators. https://databank.worldbank.org/source/world-development-indicators

Details

Retrieved 2026-07-02 from https://api.worldbank.org/v2/ (year 2022; aggregates such as regions and income groups excluded via the country metadata endpoint; only countries with complete values on all four indicators retained). One record (Central African Republic) was excluded because its retrieved 2022 life-expectancy value (18.8 years) is an evident source-data artifact.

World Bank data are published under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 license (CC BY 4.0); see https://datacatalog.worldbank.org/public-licenses.

Examples

data(wdi2022)
summary(log(wdi2022$gdp_pc))
#>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
#>   5.574   7.766   8.806   8.893   9.984  12.275
